LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF NETTA GRUTMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS IS DENIED AS MOOT. (SEE RULING ON DEFENDANTS' MOTION.) ONE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR # 7489

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, TODD MCGREGOR, JAMES JACOBS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS BROUGHT UNDER C.C.P. 437C(S), AND IS BOUND BY THE STIPULATION AND ORDER PREVIOUSLY ENTERED ("STIPULATION"). PLAINTIFF CONTENDS THAT A MOTION ARGUING DEFENDANTS' IMMUNITY IS IMPROPER UNDER THE STIPULATION. THE COURT DISAGREES: IMMUNITY IS ONE REASON FOR THE DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S POSITION THAT SHE IS ENTITLED TO A CERTAIN MINIMUM RECOVERY, WHICH ENTITLEMENT IS A SUBJECT OF THE STIPULATION. ON THE MERITS, PLAINTIFF CANNOT RECOVER DAMAGES UNDER CIVIL CODE 52(A) AGAINST DEFENDANTS. GOV'T CODE 818 PROSCRIBES THE RECOVERY OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY SUCH AS THE UC REGENTS AND PLAINTIFF CANNOT RECOVER PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST THE INDIVIDUALS BECAUSE THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT THEY WERE ACTING IN THEIR OFFICIAL CAPACITIES. JEFFERSON V. CITY OF FREMONT, 2012 U.S. DIST. LEXIS 60141 AT *7-8 (N.D.CAL. 2012); HARRIS V. CAPITAL GROWTH INVESTORS XIV, 52 CAL.3D 1142, 1172 (1991), OVERRULED ON OTHER GROUNDS, MUNSON V. DEL TACO, INC., 46 CAL.4TH 661 (2009). ONE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR # 7489

Jerome Schreibstein, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, Tel. 415.875.3355, appearing for defendant and moving party;
Ehud Gersten, Esq. of Gersten Law Group, Tel. 619.600.0098 x101, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party via court call.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, TODD MCGREGOR, JAMES JACOBS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. The motion is brought under C.C.P. 437c(s), and is bound by the stipulation and order previously entered ("Stipulation"). Plaintiff contends that a motion arguing defendants' immunity is improper under the Stipulation. The Court disagrees: immunity is one reason for the defendant's opposition to plaintiff's position that she is entitled to a certain minimum recovery, which entitlement is a subject of the Stipulation. On the merits, plaintiff cannot recover damages under Civil Code 52(a) against defendants. Gov't Code 818 proscribes the recovery of punitive damages against a public entity such as the UC Regents and plaintiff cannot recover punitive damages against the individuals because the undisputed evidence demonstrates that they were acting in their official capacities. Jefferson v. City of Fremont, 2012 U.S. Dist. LEXIS 60141 at *7-8 (N.D.Cal. 2012); Harris v. Capital Growth Investors XIV, 52 Cal.3d 1142, 1172 (1991), overruled on other grounds, Munson v. del Taco, Inc., 46 Cal.4th 661 (2009). One Order signed for both motions. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-27-2012 Denied

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF NETTA GRUTMAN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is is denied as moot. (See ruling on defendants' motion.) One order signed for both motions. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489
